seed_problem: Frontier-Eng JobShop / ALE-Bench: emit a machine schedule for a JSSP instance; checker validates precedence + no-overlap and scores 100*optimum/makespan.
mutation: composite: generalize the input from rigid job-shop (one fixed machine per operation) to a FLEXIBLE job-shop where each operation carries an eligible machine set with machine-dependent durations (participant must choose routing AND timing), and replace the unknown optimum in the score with a checker-computed always-feasible serial baseline B = sum of fastest eligible durations, so scoring is deterministic (ratio = 0.1*B/F capped at 1.0). Skinned as a reservoir dam network; small-scale instances with a bottleneck-duration difficulty ladder.
title: Reservoir Dam Network: Minimum-Makespan Release Schedule
strategies: serial baseline: run every pass alone on one global timeline, each on its fastest dam (feasible, makespan = B, ratio 0.1)
earliest-completion-time list scheduling: route each pass to the eligible dam that finishes it soonest, appended to that dam's timeline
active scheduling with gap-filling: insert operations into the earliest feasible idle window on the best dam rather than only appending
multi-restart randomized dispatch: sample many static order priorities and selection rules, keep the shortest makespan found
load-balancing routing: bias machine choice away from congested dams to reduce the makespan bottleneck
